question_text: Three forces act on a stationary box: 50 N to the right, 30 N to the left, and 20 N upwards. What is the resultant force acting horizontally on the box?
explanation: To find the resultant horizontal force, add forces in the same direction and subtract forces in opposite directions. The 50 N to the right and 30 N to the left are in opposite directions, so 50 - 30 = 20 N to the right. The 20 N upwards is vertical so it does not affect the horizontal resultant.
